hq: 325 West Huron Street, Suite 713, Chicago, IL 60654

system_description.overview: Career Bird is an AI-enabled talent development platform that helps organizations provide skill-based learning plans and career paths for their employees. It automates the creation of industry and company-specific job architectures that define roles, levels, and job families, and builds a database of skills, competencies, and responsibilities for every role.

products.Job Architecture: Automates creation and customization of a skills-based job architecture; defines responsibilities, technical skills, competencies and leadership qualities for every role; creates levels and job families
products.Skill Proficiency Survey: Surveys employees to collect self-reported proficiency; surveys managers to validate and calibrate employee proficiency
products.Career Explorer: Matches jobs based on skills, levels and job families to create career progression visualization; provides ability to compare roles based on responsibilities, skills and competencies
products.Learning Plan: Maps learning resources such as courses, articles and podcasts to skills; recommends learning resources based on skill proficiency gaps; enables employees to bookmark resources, create custom learning plans and track progress
products.Skills Intelligence: Provides understanding of what skills employees have and need to build; enables strategic workforce planning to determine how to upskill or hire talent to fill critical skill gaps

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices or servers; all cloud-based. HQ at 325 West Huron Street, Suite 713, Chicago, IL 60654 (working space managed by building management)
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (hosted in high availability data centers with multiple availability zones)
serverless: Vercel serverless functions (implied from architecture diagram)
infrastructure.cdn: Vercel Edge Network (implicit via Vercel hosting)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: System hosted in Vercel VPC environment with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs)
network_architecture.api_gateway: Firewalls configured on application gateway per CC6.6.1
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management. IDS and IPS employed via Vercel.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via Vercel to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS encryption for all web-based system requests using certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet with content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Local Development (Neo4J Desktop, Local Postgres, Local Temporal) -> GitHub -> Vercel Preview -> Vercel Production (app.careerbird.ai) -> End User. Also: Vercel -> Heroku Preview/Production (CareerBird Workers). External SaaS: OpenAI, LangSmith, ScrapeOps, Perplexity, Brave (Common SaaS Tools), Clerk (Auth Dev/Prod), AuraDB (Neo4J Preview/Prod), Supabase (Postgres Preview/Prod), Temporal (Preview/Prod Namespaces).

application_architecture.pattern: Hybrid (Vercel-hosted app + Heroku workers + multiple managed services)
languages: Not explicitly named; inferred: JavaScript/TypeScript (Next.js on Vercel), Python (Temporal workers on Heroku)
application_architecture.environments: Three environments: Local Development, Preview (Vercel Preview + Heroku Preview), Production (Vercel Production + Heroku Production). Separate database instances per environment.

data_storage.backup_frequency: Formal backup policies and procedures; suitable backups taken and maintained per Backup Policy. Specific frequency not stated.

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Clerk (Development and Production instances visible in architecture diagram)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1, CC6.6). Also required for email access and source code tool access.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination of employment
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through Vercel password policy; includes periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts. Guest/anonymous logins not allowed. Unattended desktops locked after inactivity.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest (CC6.1.5)

ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Vercel (deployment pipeline from GitHub); implied GitHub-based CI
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move changes to production requires additional approval (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Infrastructure as code implied - baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as code, configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback (CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management has set up automatic patch management with monthly patch management reviews. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned for patch compliance daily.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: LangSmith (visible in architecture diagram for AI/LLM monitoring)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions and roles (CC7.2.1)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events (CC7.2.1). Monitoring tool generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met (CC5.2.1).

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud hosting, deployment platform, edge network (subservice organization)
Worker process hosting (CareerBird Workers for Preview and Production)
Workflow orchestration engine (Preview and Production namespaces)
AI/LLM API for talent development features
Source code management and CI/CD pipeline

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software installed and configured on all production servers (continuous scan, weekly definition updates) and Mac-based workstations/laptops. Endpoint Security Solution with automatic scanning.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications, frequency adjusted as required (CC7.1.2)
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party (CC4.1.1). Results reviewed by IT management; high/critical findings remediated in timely manner.

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to facilities: Vercel is responsible for physical security of its data center; physical security controls not in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections: Career Bird does not have an office/operates from working space managed by building management; environmental security controls managed by building management/Vercel
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2: Branch protection rules requiring additional approval for production merges
CC8.1.3: Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed in SCM with MFA-protected access
CC6.1.1: MFA enforced for administrative access to production platform
CC7.4.1: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually with annual testing across different loss scenarios
CC4.1.1: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC7.1.2: Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation plans
CC6.1.3: Per-tenant data segregation - customers' access appropriately segmented from other customer accounts
CC9.2.1-CC9.2.3: Comprehensive vendor management policy with SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments for critical vendors without SOC reports

infrastructure.rationale: Vercel provides multi-AZ hosting with failover capabilities, which is good. However, there is no multi-region deployment, no named CDN beyond Vercel's built-in edge, and reliance on Vercel as a carve-out subservice means limited visibility into infrastructure controls. The use of Heroku for workers adds a secondary platform but no specific region/AZ details are given for Heroku or Supabase.

application_architecture.rationale: Hybrid architecture with Vercel (app) + Heroku (workers) + Temporal (orchestration) shows thoughtful separation of concerns. Multiple databases (Postgres via Supabase + Neo4j via AuraDB) indicate appropriate technology choices. Branch protection and code review are enforced. However, specific languages/frameworks are not explicitly named in the report, and no mention of automated testing or API style.

data_layer.rationale: Dual database strategy (relational PostgreSQL + graph Neo4j) is appropriate for a skills/career platform. Per-tenant segregation and data classification/retention policies exist. Multi-AZ via Vercel. However, no caching layer mentioned, no data warehouse, backup frequency not specified, and no file storage details.

security_posture.rationale: Annual pen testing, monthly vulnerability scanning, IDS/IPS via Vercel, MFA on admin access and source code, endpoint protection, anti-virus on all servers and workstations. Firewalls on application gateway. However, no named WAF, no named SIEM, no named endpoint protection vendor, and TLS version not specified. Cybersecurity insurance not mentioned.

devops_maturity.rationale: GitHub for source control with branch protection rules, Vercel for deployment pipeline, IaC referenced for baseline configurations, separate dev/preview/prod environments, formal change management with emergency change process. However, CI/CD tool not explicitly named beyond Vercel deploy, no container orchestration, and no specific test automation mentioned.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing conducted across different loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover via Vercel with multiple servers across availability zones. However, no RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, backup frequency not specified, and disaster recovery details are in the unaudited Section 5.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Strong vendor diversity with 12+ distinct vendors across hosting (Vercel, Heroku), databases (Supabase, AuraDB), auth (Clerk), AI (OpenAI, Perplexity), orchestration (Temporal), monitoring (LangSmith), and source control (GitHub). No single vendor concentration beyond Vercel as primary host. Vendor management policy with SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments.

overall.rationale: Career Bird demonstrates a reasonably mature tech stack for an early-stage AI startup. The architecture diagram reveals a well-thought-out multi-service design with appropriate separation of concerns. Key strengths include strong vendor diversity, per-tenant data segregation, MFA enforcement, and annual pen testing. Key gaps include no disclosed RTO/RPO, no named SIEM or WAF, limited infrastructure visibility due to Vercel carve-out, and several generic/boilerplate control descriptions that suggest template-driven SOC 2 preparation. The unqualified opinion with zero exceptions is positive but expected for a Type 1 (design-only) assessment.

red_flags: Very small engineering team (2 engineers + CTO for an AI platform handling enterprise HR data) raises bus-factor and capacity concerns
No RTO/RPO disclosed anywhere in the report despite BCDR policy existing
No named SIEM tool - unclear how security events are centrally correlated and analyzed
Several control descriptions appear boilerplate/template-driven (e.g., references to 'Windows/Network team' for patch management which seems inconsistent with a Vercel/Heroku cloud-native stack)
TLS version not specified - could be running outdated TLS versions
yellow_flags: Type 1 only (design assessment) - no operating effectiveness testing over a period
Vercel as carve-out subservice means infrastructure controls are not directly examined
No WAF mentioned despite being a web application
No named monitoring/APM tool beyond LangSmith (which is LLM-specific)
Backup frequency not specified despite formal backup policy existing
No mention of automated testing in CI/CD pipeline
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded - notable for a platform handling employee skills and career data
No specific encryption standard named (e.g., AES-256) - just 'cloud provider key management service'
green_flags: Rich architecture diagram showing clear separation of environments (Local Dev, Preview, Production)
Strong vendor diversity with 12+ purpose-specific tools rather than monolithic platform dependency
MFA enforced on admin access, email, and source code tool
Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation plans
Formal change management with branch protection, code review requirements, and emergency change process
Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including admin-privileged accounts
Access revocation within one business day of termination
BCDR testing conducted annually across different loss scenarios
Comprehensive vendor management program with SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments for non-SOC vendors
Dual database strategy (relational + graph) appropriate for skills/career domain
key_observations: The architecture diagram (p.18) is unusually transparent for a SOC 2 report, revealing specific SaaS tools (OpenAI, LangSmith, Perplexity, Brave, ScrapeOps) that are not mentioned elsewhere in the report text - this suggests the diagram is more current/honest than the boilerplate control descriptions

The report mentions 'Windows/Network team' for patch management (p.19) which is inconsistent with a fully cloud-native Vercel/Heroku stack using Mac workstations (p.47) - this is a telltale sign of template-driven SOC 2 preparation by the audit firm
Career Bird uses Temporal for workflow orchestration, which is a sophisticated choice for an early-stage startup and suggests the team has strong distributed systems experience
The use of Neo4j (graph database) alongside PostgreSQL indicates a well-considered data model for skills/career path relationships
Heroku is used specifically for 'CareerBird Workers' (likely background job processing / Temporal workers), keeping the web app on Vercel - this is a reasonable architectural separation

The report covers all 5 trust criteria in scope assertion (Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, Privacy) but then excludes Processing Integrity and Privacy as 'not relevant' - for a platform that processes employee performance and skills data, this exclusion deserves scrutiny
Team of only 6 people (CEO, CTO, UX Designer, Customer Success Manager, 2 Engineers) per org chart on p.20 - very lean for the breadth of AI/ML features described
